key_points: O'Donnell Hall has no AC — brutal for first weeks
Dorm quad assignments don't match LLC preferences
Moving out is easy if roommate issues arise
key_quotes: my roommates actually suck major ass, one has no boundaries, steals my snacks
O Donnell isn't great… my cousin got placed into another dorm by getting a doctors note saying that he needed AC
I got put into a Nuestro Hogar quad even though I never signed up for that LLC

key_points: Commuters and out-of-state students struggle to find community early
Party scene exists — alley darties, golf house, bar culture
Queer social scene is small; many closeted or heteronormative friend groups
key_quotes: I spent my freshman year at Marquette hoping to find a queer community, and did find a pretty solid group of friends, a lot of whom were queer but closeted
Abbotsford is fun. You'll meet some great friends considering its all triples
I'm from the east coast and I don't really know anyone here, so a lot of the times, I'm sitting in my room

key_points: Straz tower praised for best food and on-site gym
Marquette Cash accepted at off-campus affiliates like Qdoba
Unlimited meal swipe system in place for freshmen
key_quotes: Best food and has a gym
meal periods are just whenever the dining halls are open… marquette cash is for use on off campus affiliates like the qdoba, auntie anne's
Hit the steam, sauna, hot tub trio and all your problems disappear

key_points: No direct mental health support data surfaced in student posts
Freshman isolation and roommate conflicts go unaddressed initially
Students advised to use RA resources but tone is reactive not proactive
key_quotes: I'm a freshman at Marquette rn and so far everything has been pretty bad
you need to get out of your room. There is so much going on at your campus
i cried myself to sleep for so long. its not easy

key_points: Merit scholarships are real and often determine enrollment decisions
Tuition increases year-over-year catch students off guard
School accused of pushing lower-income families toward risky loans
key_quotes: Tuition went up… I genuinely can't even move out because if I do… I'd have to pay out of pocket
I got a full ride to Marquette
it's soooo expensive

key_points: Marquette widely listed as a strong safety with above-average teaching quality
Engineering requires Windows laptops; business school strongly recommends them too
AP credits often accepted only as electives, not fulfilling core requirements
key_quotes: They charge so much you think they would accept at least a few AP's
Check with the College you're enrolling with. Opus College of Engineering requires Windows 11 laptops
iclicker has a SUBSCRIPTION now? Thats insane

key_points: Communication delays on decisions frustrate admitted students
Financial aid packaging changes without clear explanation causes stress
Commencement speaker choices have drawn recurring student backlash
key_quotes: He told me I should be receiving an email within 24-48 hours. However, this was last Tuesday and I still have not received an email
This year I'm in a quad… I genuinely can't even move out because if I do… I'd have to pay out of pocket
Marquette picks AI expert as commencement speaker. Some students aren't happy

key_points: Campus is walking distance from downtown Milwaukee restaurants and bars
Off-campus areas north of State Street flagged as unsafe by multiple alumni
Milwaukee is a real mid-size city — urban, vibrant, but requires street awareness
key_quotes: Marquette is just across the highway from downtown Milwaukee. It's easy walking distance from countless bars, restaurants, coffee shops
Don't rent north of state
Gotta keep your head on a swivel off campus

key_points: Milwaukee ad agency scene described as 'on the decline'
Limited specific data on CS or business job placement outcomes
Full-ride recipients compete well against higher-ranked school grads per alumni anecdotes
key_quotes: The ad agencies are generally on the decline in Milwaukee
UIC has a decent CS program, and they benefit a lot from their prime location… Marquette is a great place, but UIC might have an edge for CS internships
a full ride to Marquette vs $20k/year at Northwestern — money is tight

key_points: Full-ride offers make Marquette a genuine financial win over T20 alternatives
Without strong merit aid, cost rivals more prestigious schools
AP credit policies mean students may retake courses they've already passed
key_quotes: I got a full ride to Marquette vs Northwestern — $20k a year is doable if it's the most important thing for you
Cost is $39k per year total with housing
They charge so much you think they would accept at least a few AP's

key_points: Tight-knit Jesuit community with strong sports identity, especially basketball
Midwest-heavy student body; out-of-state and international students feel the culture gap
Most students who commit seem genuinely happy — strong word-of-mouth loyalty
key_quotes: Marquette is the only answer. Friendlier community, cheaper most of the time, and students generally are much happier
I went to Marquette & my brother went to Loyola. We both made lifelong friends
I felt very out of place with my classmates

key_points: **Safety**: armed robberies and shootings reported near campus regularly
**Financial Aid**: university accused of steering low-income students toward risky loans
**LGBTQ Climate**: trans student edited post to say they decided NOT to attend after community responses
key_quotes: Our daughter and a friend were mugged at gunpoint last year at 17th & Kilbourne on their way home from the library. Case still open
Marquette University accused of pushing lower-income families toward risky loans
the replies to this post have made me feel super cynical — im probably not gonna end up here

key_points: Straz Hall includes gym, steam room, sauna, and hot tub — rare for a dorm
Les Aspin Center in DC offers a government semester program few students know about
Library has secluded upper-floor spots ideal for solo studying late at night
key_quotes: Hit the steam, sauna, hot tub trio and all your problems disappear
Anyone go to Les Aspin Center for Government in DC
anywhere past the bridge in the library — honestly one of the best parts of Marquette is how your student ID gets you in at all hours
